Interface SoundDecoder

All Known Implementing Classes:
VorbisSoundDecoder
public interface SoundDecoder
A sound decoder can decode a file, a stream of bytes, or a byte buffer into a ParsedSound (PCM data).

Currently supported formats:

  • OGG Vorbis (.ogg)

Note for Scratchers: Unlike Scratch, which supports many formats (including MP3 and WAV), we currently only support OGG Vorbis. This keeps the core lightweight and focused on free, open formats. If you have sounds in other formats that you want to use:

  1. Easy way (recommended): convert them to one of the supported formats using tools like Audacity, and they're ready to use!
  2. Advanced way: implement this interface yourself and register your custom implementation in an application context like this: 
                context.register(SoundDecoder.class, new MySoundDecoder());
    You can get the context using AppContextHolder utilities.
See Also:
  • ParsedSound

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    default String
    getFormatName()
    Returns the name of the format this decoder is used to handle, if available, otherwise a string telling the format is unknown, e.g.
    default Set<String>
    getSupportedExtensions()
    Returns all file extensions of audio formats supported by this decoder, if available, otherwise returns an empty set.
    ParsedSound
    parseBytes(ByteBuffer bb)
    Parses the data from memory into PCM.
    ParsedSound
    parseFile(Path path)
    Parses the data from file at the given path into PCM.
    default ParsedSound
    parseStream(InputStream in)
    Parses the bytes emitted by the given input stream into PCM.

  • Method Details

    • parseFile

      ParsedSound parseFile(Path path) throws IOException
      Parses the data from file at the given path into PCM.
      Parameters:
      path - the file path
      Returns:
      a new parsed sound
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O or parse error occurs
      NullPointerException - if path is null

    • parseBytes

      ParsedSound parseBytes(ByteBuffer bb) throws IOException
      Parses the data from memory into PCM. The caller must ensure that the buffer is properly prepared for reading.
      Parameters:
      bb - the loaded unparsed sound bytes
      Returns:
      a new parsed sound
      Throws:
      IOException - if a parse error occurs
      NullPointerException - if byte buffer is null

    • parseStream

      default ParsedSound parseStream(InputStream in) throws IOException
      Parses the bytes emitted by the given input stream into PCM.
      Parameters:
      in - the input stream
      Returns:
      a new parsed sound
      Throws:
      IOException - if an I/O or parse error occurs
      NullPointerException - if input stream is null
      "Implementation Note:"
      The default implementation reads all remaining bytes of the input stream into an off-heap buffer, parses them and then frees the buffer.

    • getSupportedExtensions

      default Set<String> getSupportedExtensions()
      Returns all file extensions of audio formats supported by this decoder, if available, otherwise returns an empty set.
      Returns:
      file extensions supported by this decoder (immutable)

    • getFormatName

      default String getFormatName()
      Returns the name of the format this decoder is used to handle, if available, otherwise a string telling the format is unknown, e.g. "<unknown>".
      Returns:
      the name of the format this decoder can handle, if any
